examTip: Blood Relations appears in almost every WB SI exam — 2 to 3 questions. Always draw a family tree. Never try to solve these mentally.

sections.heading: 🌳 Family Tree Method — Always Draw First
points: Step 1: Start with the person mentioned first. Place them at center.
Step 2: Add each relationship one by one using arrows.
Step 3: Male = □ (square), Female = ○ (circle), Unknown = △ (triangle)
Step 4: Horizontal line = couple (husband-wife), Vertical line = parent-child
Step 5: After drawing, trace the path between the two persons asked about.

sections.heading: ⚡ Quick Relation Shortcuts
points: Uncle → son = Cousin
Uncle → daughter = Cousin
Grandfather → son's son = Grandson
Grandmother → daughter's daughter = Granddaughter
Mother's brother = Maternal uncle (Mama)
Father's brother = Paternal uncle (Chacha/Kaka)
Father's sister = Paternal aunt (Bua/Pishi)
Mother's sister = Maternal aunt (Mausi/Mashi)

sections.heading: ⚠️ Gender Trap Words
points: ONLY MEN: Father, Son, Brother, Grandfather, Grandson, Uncle, Nephew, Husband
ONLY WOMEN: Mother, Daughter, Sister, Grandmother, Granddaughter, Aunt, Niece, Wife
NEUTRAL — look for other clues: Cousin, Sibling, Child, Parent, Grandparent, Spouse
When the problem says 'she pointed to a photograph and said...' — track whose perspective!
EXAM TRAP: 'How is A related to B?' ≠ 'How is B related to A?' — reverse gives opposite

sections.heading: 📝 Coded Blood Relation Type
points: Problem: A + B means A is father of B. A − B means A is wife of B. Find relation.
Method: Replace each symbol with its meaning and draw tree step by step.
Example: If P + Q − R, and P+Q = P is father of Q, Q-R = Q is wife of R.
→ P is father of Q. Q is wife of R. So P is father-in-law of R.
Always solve symbol by symbol — never guess or combine steps mentally.

items.question: Pointing to a woman, Ravi said 'She is the daughter of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the woman related to Ravi?
items.solution: My grandfather's only son = my father. Daughter of my father = my sister.

items.question: A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. E is D's mother. How is A related to D?
items.solution: A is sister of B. C is mother of B (so also mother of A). D is father of C. So D is A's grandfather.

items.question: Introducing a man, a woman says 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the man related to the woman?
items.solution: Only daughter of my mother = me. So his mother = me (the woman). He is her son.
